sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+ electrophilic, f– nucleophilic, f0 radical) and the dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f+.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF default).
• Geometry provenance: single-point population analyses on the fixed Cartesian coordinates supplied in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz (no further relaxation; notation DFT//input-xyz).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet for the N-electron reference; N ± 1 states were computed as doublets for f±).
• Software: PySCF 2.x (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill).
• Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for N, N+1, and N–1 states all converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true. The engine emitted three warnings, reproduced verbatim above. No fall-backs were triggered.

Reproducibility
Command (from result.cli_invocation):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py \
      fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 \
      /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz \
      --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpztqlvy09/live.json
Live engine output was streamed to a file named fukui_<timestamp>.out in the working directory during execution; the JSON result and plotted Fukui surfaces are saved at the paths shown in the result object.

Key numerical results
• Atom count: 14
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (0-based index 2, carbonyl carbon)  f+ = 0.39938969
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (0-based index 0, carbonyl oxygen)  f– = 0.23839906
Complete f+/f–/f0 vectors and the dual descriptor are available in the output JSON.
